facts.shortSummary: Four newly awakened prisoners break out of their crude cell, arm themselves from the abandoned smithy, and settle on an ambush after overhearing drunk goblin guards nearby.
facts.longSummary: Acescale, Gaudin, Loria, and Dilion awaken in a rough prison built inside an abandoned smithy on the grounds of Perdoli Manor in Western Cymea. After introducing themselves and probing the cell, they discover a rotten plank wall, crawl into the workshop, gather improvised tools and gear, overhear drunk goblin guards complaining about Ulgna, Folcan, and Grimstone, and inspect a tidier side room full of scavenged clothing and armor before committing to an attack on the guards.

location.context: crude prison cell, abandoned smithy, and adjoining storage room
location.notes: The party begins the session imprisoned inside a makeshift cell built within the manor's former workshop.

npcs.name: Folcan
npcs.context: goblin leader named by the guards
npcs.notes: The drunken guards complain that Folcan listens to Ulgna.

npcs.name: Ulgna
npcs.context: goblin superior blamed by the guards
npcs.notes: The guards frame their punishment posting as fallout from Ulgna and her allies drawing Grimstone's ire.

npcs.name: Grimstone
npcs.notes: The guards say the prisoners are being kept for Grimstone.

npcs.name: Morky
npcs.context: drunk goblin guard at the card game
npcs.notes: One of the unseen guards accuses Morky of cheating.

items.name: smith's tools
items.notes: Dilion scavenges smith's tools from the abandoned workshop.

items.name: tinker's tools
items.notes: Acescale finds usable tinker's tools among the workshop debris.

items.name: magical formal outfit
items.notes: Acescale detects transmutation magic on an old but pristine gentleman's dinner outfit in the side room.

facts.shortSummary: The party's clumsy two-door ambush still succeeds, and the three drunken jailer goblins are killed in the forge-side guard room.
facts.longSummary: After discovering one of the two doors into the goblin room is barred, the party improvises a distraction and bursts into the first fight of the session. The goblin jailers are drunk but armed, and the brawl turns messy with darkness, acid, arrows, and Loria's brutal work at the knees, yet the group keeps the noise contained and kills the three guards before the rest of the manor notices.

location.context: guard room beside the smithy prison
location.notes: The fight happens in the adjoining room where the goblin guards had been drinking and gambling.

npcs.name: Morky
npcs.notes: One of the three jailer goblins killed in the fight.

npcs.name: Norrica
npcs.notes: One of the three jailer goblins defeated in the guard room.

npcs.name: Skelver
npcs.notes: One of the three jailer goblins defeated in the guard room.

items.name: Loria's short swords
items.notes: The goblin guards had been carrying Loria's stolen weapons.

mainEnemies.name: Morky, Norrica, and Skelver
mainEnemies.notes: Three drunk goblin jailers guarding the forge-side prison room.

combat.notes: The party springs the ambush in the guard room and wipes out the three prison guards before the wider estate is alerted.

facts.shortSummary: With the first guards dead, the party loots back their gear, rests in the smithy, and surveys the grounds and nearby barracks of Perdoli Manor before choosing a stealthier next move.
facts.longSummary: Once the immediate danger passes, the group searches the goblin room and recovered stores, reclaiming weapons, cards, food, and scattered personal gear. They step outside long enough to understand that they are on the edge of sprawling Perdoli Manor, with gardens, hedges, a distant main villa, and a nearby barracks, then settle in for a short rest, a level-up pause, and a longer planning discussion about whether to leave, hide, or probe the goblins further.

location.context: smithy, gear room, and overgrown grounds outside the prison building
location.notes: The beat expands the party's understanding of the manor layout while they recover and plan.

npcs.name: Grimstone
npcs.notes: The group's recovered information and discussion reinforce that the prisoners are being held for Grimstone inside the manor.

items.name: Acescale's playing cards
items.notes: The cards taken by the goblins are recovered from the guard room.

items.name: silver ring with weathered pearl
items.notes: Gaudin reclaims a silver ring set with a pearl that had been glowing faintly.

items.name: stolen rations and brandy
items.notes: The goblin guard room contains scavenged food, cheap apple brandy, and picked-over prisoner supplies.

facts.shortSummary: The party stages a fake prisoner transfer outside the barracks, using Gaudin's goblin disguise and Loria's telepathic translation, but Folcan grows suspicious and the deception starts to collapse.
facts.longSummary: Hoping to learn more without attacking a full barracks, the party builds a complicated ruse: Gaudin disguises himself as a goblin, Loria pretends to be bound, and the others hide behind a hedge with the steel defender ready. The approach works just long enough to pull barracks goblins out onto the path, but Folcan proves sharper than the first drunk guards, dismisses the act, and begins punishing the supposed troublemakers, forcing the party toward open action.

location.context: hedgerow path between the smithy and the nearby barracks
location.notes: The party tries to exploit cover near the barracks rather than storming the building directly.

npcs.name: Folcan
npcs.notes: Folcan steps out from the barracks and immediately reads the scene better than the party hoped.

npcs.name: Ulgna
npcs.context: superior goblin leader back in the barracks
npcs.notes: Folcan orders that Ulgna be told to send help if he does not return.

npcs.name: Morky
npcs.context: drunk goblin used as the cover story
npcs.notes: Folcan frames the scene as Morky being dealt with for drinking too much.

facts.shortSummary: Loria's fog cloud turns the failed bluff into a fast street fight, and the party kills the barracks guards while taking Folcan alive before the rest of the goblins can respond.
facts.longSummary: The moment the bluff fails, Loria drops a fog cloud across the path and hedgerow, cutting sight lines and throwing the barracks-side goblins into confusion. Fighting through the obscuring mist, the party tears down the nearby guards with spells, steel defender pressure, and Loria's blades, then hauls the surviving Folcan back toward the smithy before the wider barracks can react to the noise.

location.context: fog-shrouded path and hedgerow outside the barracks
location.notes: The fight stays outside the barracks itself and ends quickly enough to avoid a full alarm.

npcs.name: Folcan
npcs.notes: Folcan is beaten, captured alive, and dragged back for interrogation.

mainEnemies.name: Folcan and barracks goblins
mainEnemies.notes: The party fights Folcan and the goblin guards he brought onto the path from the barracks.

combat.notes: The fog cloud and hedgerow let the party finish the skirmish before the full barracks is mobilized.

facts.shortSummary: Interrogating the captured Folcan, the party learns how Grimstone's prisoner racket works and where hidden tunnels might lead into the manor's wine cellar.
facts.longSummary: Back at the smithy, the party revives and threatens Folcan until he finally explains the arrangement: Grimstone, masked in white marble and black robes, takes one prisoner a week from the manor while the goblins keep silver, copper, and lesser spoils. Folcan also reveals that about ten goblins plus Ulgna remain in the barracks, that hobgoblins guard the main approach, and that a tunnel network may connect the barracks, the kitchen, and perhaps the ruined chapel to the manor's wine cellar, giving the group a plausible infiltration route for next session.

location.context: smithy and roadside cover near the barracks
location.notes: The party questions Folcan back near the prison building after the second fight.

npcs.name: Folcan
npcs.notes: Folcan gives up the tunnel information and explains the goblins' deal with Grimstone.

npcs.name: Grimstone
npcs.notes: Folcan describes Grimstone as a figure in black robes with a white marble mask who takes prisoners into the manor.

npcs.name: Ulgna
npcs.context: other goblin leader in the barracks
npcs.notes: Folcan complains about Ulgna while describing the goblin hierarchy and the surviving force in the barracks.
